1.text: Leaves most often opposite; fruit berry-like.

2.text: Calyx entirely closed or lobed in the bud; pedicels 1-7-flowered, dichotomous (3 flowers or more); fruit with numerous seeds.

2'.text: Sepals free, sometimes very small; fruit monospermate.

3.text: Petals free; flowers axillary, fasciculate or racemose, sometimes solitary; cotyledons fused..... 3. Eugenia.

3'.text: Petals most often coherent; inflorescences in terminal cymes or sometimes lateral; cotyledons free..... 2. Syzygium.

1'.text: Leaves alternate, at least on the flowering branches; fruit capsular; introduced species.

4.text: Stamens in 5 bundles opposite to sepals; in spikes; petals free..... 4. Melaleuca.

4'.text: Stamens free, or almost; petals coherent, forming an opercule; sepals very small..... 5. Eucalyptus.

species_psidium.name: KEY TO SPECIES INTRODUCED IN WEST AFRICA

1.text: Calyx lobed in the bud; leaves obovate, glabrous.. 1. Ps. cattleyanum.

2.text: Leaves and twigs glabrous, young twigs quadrangular. Ps. Friedrichstahlianum.

3.text: Young twigs quadrangular, lateral leaf nerves 12-16 pairs impressed above..... 2. Ps. guajava.

3'.text: Young twigs rounded, lateral leaf nerves 7-10 pairs not impressed above..... Ps. guineense.

2.text: Leaves subsessile, cordate at the base, elongated, 10-25 cm long; flowers large: adult flower buds 8-10 mm in diam. 2. S. Gilletii.

2'.text: Leaves petiolate, acute, obtuse or rounded at the base, smaller; flowers smaller: flower buds 2-5 mm in diam.

3.text: Young twigs quadrangular; leaves rather small, 4-7 cm long; sepals barely developed..... 1. S. Staudti.

4.text: Distinct sepals, also on the fruit, subimbricate in the bud, 1 mm tall; obovate leaves, long cuneate at the base, shortly acuminate at the apex.... 3. S. congolense.

5.text: Swamp tree, with stilt roots; ovate-elliptic leaves, often rounded at the base, long acuminate at the apex and very sharp..... 4. S. owariense.

5'.text: Trees of non-swampy habitats, without stilt roots; leaves acute at the base..... 5. S. guineense s. l.

6.text: Shrub or small tree of the seashore; ovate-elliptic leaves, extremely coriaceous, rounded or obtuse, sometimes shortly acuminate at the apex; petiole short and thick, approximately 5 mm long..... 5 c. var. littorale.

6'.text: Trees or shrubs of savannas or forest gallery; petiole longer than above.

7.text: Savanna tree or shrub; petiole 2-4 cm long; obovate leaves, often rounded or obtuse at the apex; inflorescences often on defoliate branches..... 5 b. var. macrocarpon.

7'.text: Forest gallery tree; petiole 0.5-2 cm long; acuminate leaves, persistent; inflorescences always terminal..... 5 a. var. guineae

1'.text: Cultivated species, sometimes naturalized; petals often free.

8.text: Petals fused, capping the floral bud, detaching all together.

9.text: Ovary cylindric, somewhat quadrangular; sepals triangular; inflorescences terminal..... 6. S. aromaticum.

9'.text: Ovary obconic; sepals scarcely developed; inflorescences lateral, on the defoliate parts of the branches... 7. S. cumini.

8'.text: Petals free, fairly large, 1-2 cm long.

10.text: Inflorescence on old wood; flowers red... 9. S. malaccense.

10'.text: Inflorescence terminal; flowers white; leaves narrow lance-shaped..... 8. S. jambos.

1.text: Indigenous species; pedicels rarely exceeding 1 cm in length, most often less than 5 mm.

2.text: Leaves subsessile, narrowly rounded or subcordate at the base, 17-28 cm long; flowers subsessile..... 1. E. gabonensis.

2'.text: Leaves petiolate, acute or obtuse at the base, smaller.

3.text: Young branches glabrous, then scaly; leaves fairly large, 12-17 cm long, glabrous; petiole short, soon thick; ovary glabrous..... 2. E. Klainea

3'.text: Young branches pubescent, not becoming scaly; leaves smaller.

6.text: Leaves with marginal nerves almost as strong as the midvein, somewhat impressed above, prominent below; blade up to 5 cm wide.

6'.text: Leaves with marginal nerves poorly marked, not impressed above.

7.text: Leaves 4-5 cm in length..... 13. E. sp.

7'.text: Leaves 5-10 cm in length..... 7. E. ogoouensis.

8.text: Leaves lanceolate, obtuse; 3.5-6 cm long, 0.5-1.5 cm wide; flowers subsessile..... 4. E. Tholloni.

9.text: Flowers subsessile; petioles fairly slender, 2-5 mm long. 6. E. obanensis.

10.text: Flowers fasciculate; petioles very short, about 2 mm long..... 8. E. fernandopona.

10'.text: Flowers in short racemes, with distinct bracts; petiole about 4 mm long..... 9. E. librevillensis.

1'.text: Introduced species; pedicels long; edible fruits.

11.text: Fruits red, lobed; sepals oblong-linear, up to ± 4 mm long..... 5. E. uniflora.

11'.text: Fruits smooth; sepals lanceolate or oblong, up to approximately 1 cm long.

12.text: Sepals lanceolate, foliaceous; leaves pubescent above.

12'.text: Sepals oblong; leaves glabrous..... 11. E. brasiliensis.
